faqDescription: Frequently asked questions about the festival experience.

travelSections.title: Public Transport
travelSections.description:
Here are the best ways to get there, with special considerations for weekend travel:

- From Graz (Closest Major Hub & Airport)
Graz is the nearest large city to Fehring, making it the easiest transit point for anyone flying in or arriving by long-distance bus.

By Train: Attendees can take a direct S-Bahn (Line S3) or an ÖBB Regional Express (REX) train from Graz Hauptbahnhof (Graz Hbf) or Graz-Liebenau Murpark directly to the Fehring station.

Duration: The journey takes between 50 and 60 minutes.

Saturday Considerations: Trains run reliably on Saturdays, typically operating hourly. However, early morning and late-night services are less frequent on weekends, so attendees should double-check their specific arrival window.

- From Vienna (Main International Hub)
For international guests arriving at Vienna International Airport (VIE) or Vienna Central Station (Wien Hbf), there are a couple of scenic and comfortable routes.

Train via Graz: The most straightforward rail option is taking the high-speed Railjet from Wien Hbf to Graz Hbf (about 2.5 hours), and then transferring to the regional train to Fehring. This takes about 4 hours in total.

Train via Wiener Neustadt: Alternatively, attendees can take a train from Vienna to Wiener Neustadt Hbf, and then transfer to a regional train that goes directly to Fehring.

Bus Options: The Verbund Linie bus operates from Vienna (Südtiroler Platz) to Fürstenfeld, where you can catch a short connection to Fehring. This takes around 3.5 hours.

Saturday Considerations: While the mainline Railjet trains from Vienna to Graz operate regularly throughout the weekend, the regional train and bus connections to Fehring operate on reduced weekend schedules. Guests should align their Vienna departures carefully with the local Fehring connections to avoid long layovers.


Important Tips for the Weekend:

Use the ÖBB Scotty App: I highly recommend telling attendees to download the ÖBB app (or use the ÖBB Scotty website). It provides the most accurate, up-to-the-minute schedules for Saturday regional travel, platform changes, and ticket prices.

Last Mile: Kasernenstraße is fairly central in Fehring, but if attendees are carrying heavy festival gear, it might be worth checking if local taxis need to be booked in advance from the Fehring train station, as rural station taxis are often sparse on weekends.

travelSections.title: Car
travelSections.description:
If you arrive by car, please consider carpooling and follow the on-site parking guidance from the festival team.

You can reach to us for carpooling...we have a Whatsapp group dedicated for that

travelSections.title: Camper
travelSections.description:
- We do have a camper park but we also have limited spots.
- Sleeping in your own Camper is free. (Please select it anyway so we can plan and organize the space :))
- There will be no utility in the camper park so be ready.
- The camper park is close to the bar and gym so it can be a little bit noisy.

supportDescription: If you would like to support us, every donation helps us keep participation accessible and inclusive. Your contribution directly supports safer spaces, workshop materials, and community-led programming.
partnersDescription: Supporters and collaborators of the festival.
note: Ticket sales are managed on Pretix.
termsDescription: Privacy and policy documents will be available here soon.
heroLead: FLINTA+ is an acronym that stands for Frauen (women), Lesben (lesbians), Inter*, Nichtbinär (non-binary), Trans, and Agender. It’s primarily used in Germany. The asterisk (*) is sometimes added to highlight that this is not an exhaustive list and that the term includes all gender identities and expressions that are not cisgender men
contactDescription: Reach out for partnerships, accessibility requests, or ticket support.

faqs.question: Accommodation Options
faqs.answer:
Camping & Caravans (Included / Free)
Please note: Even though camping is free, you still need to "purchase" a free ticket during registration so we can plan the space!

🏕️ Standard Tent Camping
Pitch your tent right in the action! This area is close to the bar and gym, meaning you’ll be right in the middle of the festival buzz.
(Heads up: You will hear party noise at night and general convention noise during the day.)

🌲 Quiet Tent Camping
Need your beauty sleep? We have a new quiet camping area located just a 15-minute walk away via a beautiful forest path. It’s the perfect spot to retreat and recharge.

🚐 Caravans & Camper Vans
Sleeping in your own rig is totally free!
(Heads up: We do not have electricity hookups or greywater stations on-site. Please arrive fully charged, empty your tanks beforehand, and come prepared!)

Indoor Rooms (Extra Charge)
Want to travel light? We have a limited number of indoor rooms available in the main building, just a 5-minute walk from the main action (and noticeably quieter than the main campsite!).

All indoor rooms include blankets, sheets, and pillows. (Please note: The indoor rooms are unfortunately not wheelchair accessible.)

🛏️ Shared Bedroom — €70.00
A comfortable shared space with a maximum of 8 beds. Your ticket secures one spot in the room.

🤫 Shared "Quiet" Bedroom — €70.00
A shared room (max 8 beds) specifically reserved for attendees who want a guaranteed quiet space to sleep or retreat to during the day. Your ticket secures one spot.

🛌 Double Bedroom (One Spot) — €110.00
A room featuring one double bed. This ticket secures one spot in the bed.

🚪 Single Bedroom — €150.00
Your very own private room in the main building.

faqs.question: Food & Drink Options
faqs.answer:
🍽️ Food & Dining at Kunterbunt This Year

As our festival continues to grow, we are upgrading our food setup! To make room for some exciting new catering options, we’ve had to make a few important changes to our communal areas.

What to Expect
Breakfast is on Us: A basic breakfast is included in all convention tickets!

The Snack Bar: We are introducing a new snack bar where you can grab tea, coffee, and light, pre-prepared snacks throughout the day. In Snack bar, donations are recommended  so please bring some cash with you :)

Hot Meals: We will be serving hot, freshly prepared meals (available via the Food Ticket—see below!).

⚠️ Important Changes to Communal Spaces
Because we need to dedicate our space to the new hot meal service and snack bar, there will be NO common fridge and NO shared kitchen this year.

Please come prepared with your own camping cooking gear, just as you would for other outdoor conventions. (Camping stoves are absolutely allowed!)

Supermarkets & Supplies
Local supermarkets are just a 15-minute walk away. However, because the convention takes place at the height of summer and our beautiful site is located up a hill, we strongly recommend doing your grocery shopping before you arrive.

If you do need to make a supply run into town during the festival, we will have a small number of community bikes available to borrow.

🎟️ The Food Ticket
Want to take the hassle out of cooking? Let us feed you!

The Deal: The Food Ticket includes one hearty, fully vegetarian/vegan hot meal per day from Friday to Monday (4 hot meals in total).

All or Nothing: When it comes to meals at Kunterbunt, you're either all in or all out! We do not sell tickets for individual meals.

The Chefs: Every meal is made with love by our incredible Portuguese chef, Filipa, alongside our amazing team of volunteers!

(Food Tickets can be purchased at checkout alongside your convention pass).
